role: A theme-and-motion preference control panel that exposes three pill-toggle rows for typography, color pigment, and motion feel, applying choices via :root data-attributes and a React prop callback.
capabilities: Render three labeled pill-toggle rows (type, pigment, motion feel)
Apply selected type/pigment as data-type/data-pigment on document.documentElement
Delegate motion feel selection to parent via setFeel prop
Provide an internal reusable ToggleRow subcomponent
Stamp a ui://controls/taste address for instrumentation
substance_why: The component is interactive and wired to real DOM side effects, yet the file header explicitly labels it a 'SLICE-ONLY SCAFFOLD (S4)' meant for flipping and watching rather than final spec implementation.
interactions_present: Click a type pill to update local state and set data-type on :root
Click a pigment pill to update local state and set data-pigment on :root
Click a motion feel pill to call setFeel with 'spring' or 'eased'
interactions_missing: No keyboard/ARIA toggle semantics (aria-pressed, role=radiogroup, arrow-key navigation)
No persistence, URL sync, or restoration of type/pigment preferences
No system-preference detection or defaulting for motion or color scheme
No visual preview, reset-to-default, or disabled/loading states
No type-safe mapping from string ids to MotionFeel beyond an unsafe cast
No label text for the motion row beyond a glyph
performance_risks: Direct document.documentElement.setAttribute on every click may trigger broad CSS selector invalidation if many rules depend on data-type/data-pigment
Unmemoized ToggleRow and pill buttons re-render on every Taste state change even when unrelated rows change
reuse_verdict_why: The 'taste' concept and the three preference axes are worth keeping, but the scaffold implementation should be rebuilt with proper state management, accessibility, and token-driven styling.
decomposition_hints: Extract ToggleRow into a shared, accessible pill/radio-group primitive in a components folder
Move type/pigment constants and default values into a tokens/preferences module
Lift DOM attribute mutation into a theme provider or hook so Taste remains pure UI
Separate motion feel control into its own component if it is consumed independently elsewhere
Introduce a preference store/hook that handles persistence, defaults, and system-preference syncing
